source_pr.title: planner: for UNION statements, ORDER BY cannot use column references including a table name
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? fix #8196 ### What is changed and how it works? * Remove the source ``col.TblName`` when building the columns of ``Union`` * For ``UNION`` or ``UNION ALL``, ``ORDER BY`` cannot use column references including a table name (that is, names in ``tbl_name.col_name`` format). See: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/union.html * These ``UNION`` statements should return an error if ``ORDER BY`` uses column references with a table name ### Check List Tests - Unit test Code changes - Has changed the impl. Side effects - None Related changes - None --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/8389)

symptoms: _, err = tk.Exec("(select a,b from t1 limit 2) union all (select a,b from t2 order by a limit 1) order by t1.b") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: _, err = tk.Exec("(select a,b from t1 limit 2) union all (select a,b from t2 order by a limit 1) order by t1.b") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: _, err = tk.Exec("(select a,b from t1 limit 2) union all (select a,b from t2 order by a limit 1) order by t1.b") (executor/executor_test.go)
